inclusion_criteria: Population: Undergraduate dental students (preclinical or clinical); mixed samples only if data for this group are separable
Intervention: Team-based learning (TBL) used as the primary instructional approach
Comparator: Another teaching pedagogy (e.g., lectures, problem-based learning, enquiry-based learning, case-based learning), with parallel or historical controls

Outcomes/design: Reports quantitative student satisfaction and/or academic performance enabling between-group comparisons (empirical comparative designs such as RCTs, quasi-experiments, or controlled pre–post studies)
exclusion_criteria: Wrong population: postgraduate learners, non-dental health professions, or mixed samples where undergraduate dental data are not isolatable
Wrong intervention: TBL not used or only a minor/indistinguishable component of a blended model
No appropriate comparator: single-arm, descriptive, or qualitative-only studies without comparison to another pedagogy
Wrong outcomes/design: does not report quantitative student satisfaction or academic performance; non-empirical articles (reviews, editorials, protocols)
screening_criteria.description: Select empirical comparative studies evaluating team-based learning versus other pedagogies among undergraduate dental students, reporting quantitative outcomes on student satisfaction and/or academic performance.

included_articles.title: Effects of team-based learning on fixed prosthodontic education in a Japanese School of Dentistry.
Team-based learning versus traditional lectures in local anesthesia course: A cross-sectional study exploring dental students' perception and performance.
Comparison between flipped classroom and team-based learning in fixed prosthodontic education.
